name: Successful Inbound Connection from a Known Botnet CandC
notes: This rule triggers when the system detects a successful connection from a Botnet command and control host.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device
conditions_text: when the context is Remote to Local
when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device
when the source IP is a part of any of the following BOT.BOT_Control
when the destination port is one of the following 25, 53
TD_list: APPLY Successful Inbound Connection from a Known Botnet CandC on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the context is Remote to Local
AND when a flow or an event matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Successful Communication, BB:CategoryDefinition: Firewall or ACL Accept Event for a FW/Router/Switch Device
AND when the source IP is a part of any of the following BOT.BOT_Control
AND NOT when the destination port is one of the following 25, 53
RED_list: Event name: Successful Inbound Connection from a Known Botnet CandC
Event description: Successful connection inbound from a known botnet command and control host.  Local host should be considered suspicious.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Destination IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
